name: Mitochondrial Biogenesis and Dynamics Registry Metadata Refresh

scope.question: Do current ClinicalTrials.gov and PubMed checks for stale Mitochondrial Biogenesis and Dynamics trial-watch records change the public outlook or only clear local registry metadata debt?

summary: Ran a bounded Mitochondrial Biogenesis and Dynamics registry metadata refresh after the scoped trial-watch audit showed three registry-linked studies, two stale or missing registry checks, and zero open stale watch items. Direct ClinicalTrials.gov API checks refreshed the exercise-training registry NCT01477164, STRIDE mavodelpar NCT04535609, and the SHAPE elamipretide watchlist record NCT07275424. The completed local exercise and STRIDE records remain represented by existing result-bearing sources, SHAPE remains recruiting with no posted results, and same-window PubMed plus broad registry searches did not find a new older-adult functional or druglike mitochondrial-biogenesis/dynamics result that changes the public outlook. A 2025 same-registry exercise multi-omics paper and several close no-results or preclinical records are recorded as future coverage-repair context rather than a current metadata-refresh public change.

materiality_decision.rationale: This pass cleared stale local trial-details metadata for existing scoped records. The current registry and PubMed checks do not add new efficacy, safety, durability, disease, mortality, or broad aging evidence beyond already represented structured-exercise and STRIDE boundary results. The 2025 NCT01477164-linked phosphoproteome/acetylproteome publication and older no-results registry leads are source-completeness context for a future coverage-repair pass, not same-window field movement.

search_log.summary: Scoped report contained 3 registry-linked studies, 0 missing trial-detail records, 2 stale or missing registry checks, 0 open stale watch items, and 1 pending SHAPE watchlist record.

search_log.query: Direct checks for NCT01477164, NCT04535609, and NCT07275424
search_log.summary: NCT01477164 remains completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section and last update posted August 8, 2017; NCT04535609 remains completed with posted ClinicalTrials.gov results first posted April 3, 2024 and last update posted May 8, 2024; NCT07275424 remains recruiting with no posted results, last update posted December 10, 2025, estimated primary completion March 15, 2026, and estimated study completion April 15, 2026.

search_log.query: (mitochondrial biogenesis OR mitochondrial dynamics OR PGC-1alpha OR PGC-1a OR elamipretide OR mavodelpar OR REN001) AND (aging OR older OR elderly OR frailty OR sarcopenia OR exercise), Entrez dates 2026/06/05 to 2026/06/14
search_log.summary: Broad same-window PubMed search returned exercise, mitochondrial quality-control, sarcopenia, disease-model, review, and wrong-scope context. The close Pyr-Apelin-13 sarcopenia paper was aged-mouse preclinical delivery work rather than controlled older-human functional evidence; no elamipretide, mavodelpar, or older-adult mitochondrial-biogenesis/dynamics result changed the public read.

search_log.query: NCT01477164 OR NCT04535609 OR NCT07275424, all dates
search_log.summary: Known-registry PubMed search returned two NCT01477164-linked papers: PMID 40595512 from 2025 and PMID 36147777 from 2022. They predate this delta window and should be considered in a future coverage-repair or source-completeness pass rather than folded into this registry metadata refresh.

search_log.query: elamipretide aging older adults physical function; mavodelpar REN001 PPAR-delta mitochondrial myopathy; mitochondrial biogenesis older adults exercise; mitochondrial dynamics older adults exercise; PGC-1alpha older adults trial; mitochondrial function sarcopenia older adults intervention
search_log.summary: Broad registry searches returned SHAPE, older or adjacent exercise/nutrition/disease registries, NAD/redox records, sarcopenia or cancer-exercise context, and no-results records such as NCT07479940, NCT07613619, NCT06539078, and NCT06420726. No newly posted result-bearing registry changed the mitochondrial biogenesis and dynamics outlook.

trial_watch_checks.summary: The exercise-training registry remains completed with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section. The peer-reviewed PMID 28273480 result remains the public evidence source, and month-only registry dates are metadata context rather than public interpretation changes.

STRIDE remains completed with posted ClinicalTrials.gov results and no current registry movement. Local metadata now records the actual primary completion date, results first-posted date, and June 14 registry check.
SHAPE remains recruiting with no ClinicalTrials.gov results section. The small open-label healthy-aging elamipretide pilot remains a watchlist item, not efficacy evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Microneedle patch-based delivery of Pyr-Apelin-13 reverses functional and structural deficits in age-related sarcopenia.
excluded_sources.reason: Same-window aged-mouse Pyr-Apelin-13 delivery study with mitochondrial-DNA and grip-strength signals. It is preclinical sarcopenia context and does not change the current older-human exercise-anchored public read.

excluded_sources.title: Mechanisms by Which Exercise Delays Brain Aging Through Regulation of the Mitochondrial Quality Control System.
excluded_sources.reason: Review-level exercise and mitochondrial-quality-control context; it does not provide a new controlled older-adult mitochondrial-biogenesis/dynamics intervention result.

excluded_sources.title: Excessive training does not induce mitochondrial dysfunction or impair insulin signalling within skeletal muscle.
excluded_sources.reason: Same-window healthy trained-athlete overtraining study, not an older-adult, aging, frailty, sarcopenia, or druglike mitochondrial-biogenesis/dynamics result.

excluded_sources.title: Endurance exercise improves age- and obesity-related cardiac dysfunction and locomotor impairment through muscle Ggamma1 gene in Drosophila.
excluded_sources.reason: Drosophila exercise-aging study with mitochondrial-pathway language; it is preclinical and does not alter the current human exercise baseline.

excluded_sources.title: Aerobic and resistance exercise-regulated phosphoproteome and acetylproteome modifications in human skeletal muscle.
excluded_sources.reason: NCT01477164-linked 2025 human multi-omics paper that may matter for source completeness, but it predates the surveillance window and should be evaluated through a future coverage-repair/source-completeness pass rather than this registry metadata refresh.

excluded_sources.title: Skeletal Muscle Mitochondria in Ageing
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting no-results mitochondrial-aging registry returned by broad search; useful future coverage context, but not a same-window result-bearing public update.

excluded_sources.title: Obesity and Aging: Impacts of Nutritional Counseling and Physical Exercise on Telomeres and Senescence Parameters
excluded_sources.reason: Active-not-recruiting no-results nutrition/exercise aging registry with telomere and senescence endpoints; not a result-bearing mitochondrial-biogenesis/dynamics update.

notes: This pass was selected manually from the global stale registry-check report after the State of the Field packet showed no open approval items and the July edition remained date-blocked.

The completed local studies remain posted-result context because their result-bearing sources are already represented; a missing ClinicalTrials.gov results section does not make those public result records pending.

PMID 40595512 is likely worth a separate source-completeness look because it is linked to NCT01477164, but it was not staged here because this pass was scoped to current registry metadata and same-window deltas.
next_actions: Regenerate the scoped and global trial-watch reports so the State of the Field blocker reflects the refreshed mitochondrial-biogenesis metadata.

Consider a future Mitochondrial Biogenesis and Dynamics coverage-repair pass for NCT01477164-linked post-translational-modification papers and older no-results registry context if source completeness is prioritized.
Continue the stale registry metadata queue with the next coherent track-level batch rather than mixing unrelated tracks in one session.
